items.question: Are AIHA and PCHS partners or affiliates?
items.answer: No. AIHA is contracting with PCHS to bring the mobile clinic to the island. AIHA is not a medical provider.

items.question: Are AIHA or PCHS making money from this program?
items.answer: No. PCHS mobile units operate at a loss, and AIHA anticipates needing alternative revenue such as grants to sustain services.

items.question: What alternative revenue sources are you exploring?
items.answer: Private and public grants are in progress. If successful, we may consider a referendum for steady funding in the future.

items.question: If I don't like the PCHS provider, can I switch?
items.answer: Yes. You can seek care elsewhere at any time. Note that primary care wait times elsewhere can be six months or longer.

items.question: Why is the van only here two days a month?
items.answer: Funding. Initial private contributions will support two days per month. Additional donations or grants will expand availability.

items.question: Will AIHA have access to my medical information?
items.answer: No. The contract prohibits sharing identifiable information with AIHA. PCHS may share aggregate, de-identified data for program evaluation.

items.question: Can I volunteer?
items.answer: Yes, please! Email [info@andersonislandhealth.org](mailto:info@andersonislandhealth.org). For medical volunteering, [contact PCHS directly](https://www.pchsweb.org/about/volunteering/).
